reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by broadcasting a data column sidecar with parent_root on a non-finalized fork that exists in fork choice but conflicts with finalized checkpoint ancestry, causing honest Prysm nodes (31% of network) to accept and propagate invalid sidecars that may lead to invalid block reconstruction. Severity downgraded from Critical to High because single-client bug (Prysm at 31% network share) cannot directly affect >50% threshold required for Critical, though gossip propagation may indirectly affect additional nodes.
